text:
# Plan 002: Build the Stage 1 bounded source-inspection loop

> **Executor instructions**: Read this entire plan before editing. Follow each
> step in order. Run every verification command and confirm the expected result
> before continuing. Stop on any condition in the STOP section. Do not invent a
> workaround or expand the tool catalogue.
>
> **Required writer model**: The only writer must be `deepseek-v4-pro` inside a
> fresh isolated worktree created from the accepted Plan 001 commit. Three fresh
> independent `deepseek-v4-flash` reviewers inspect the same candidate commit.
> No model aliases, fallback, or substitution are allowed.
>
> **Evidence gate**: Plan 001 must be accepted first. Its delivery report must
> show that Job 328 could not be judged honestly from the title-and-URL stub and
> that source inspection was the next useful check. If that evidence is absent,
> stop. Do not build Stage 1 merely because this file exists.
>
> **Drift check (run first after Plan 001 is accepted)**:
>
> ```bash
> git diff --stat <accepted-plan-001-sha>..HEAD -- \
>   src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
>   src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces src/haxjobs/cli.py \
>   tests discussion/fixtures/harness diagram docs/implementation-reports
> git status --short -- \
>   src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
>   src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces src/haxjobs/cli.py \
>   tests discussion/fixtures/harness diagram docs/implementation-reports
> ```
>
> Expected: HEAD equals the accepted Plan 001 candidate or contains only
> explicitly reviewed changes. No in-scope unowned changes. On mismatch, stop
> and ask for plan reconciliation.

## Status

- **Priority**: P1
- **Effort**: L
- **Risk**: MED
- **Depends on**: `plans/001-build-stage-0-observed-job-review.md`
- **Category**: direction, architecture, security, tests
- **Planned at**: commit `5423187`, 2026-07-17; must be restamped to the accepted Plan 001 SHA before execution

## Why this matters

Stage 0 deliberately leaves Job 328 unresolved because the stored evidence is only a title and LinkedIn URL. Stage 1 tests one change: can Hax ask normal code to inspect the trusted source, receive a bounded evidence result, then answer without inventing what the source did not prove?

This plan extends the Stage 0 runtime into the first real agent loop:

```text
model -> one validated tool call -> structured result -> model -> final answer
```

Only `inspect_job_source(job_ref)` is active. The model never supplies an arbitrary URL. A blocked, missing, or unavailable source is still a valid result.

## Decided product contract

The executor must preserve these decisions:

- Stage 1 extends the accepted Stage 0 runner. There is one loop.
- The only registered and active tool is `inspect_job_source(job_ref)`.
- The tool accepts a trusted job reference, not a URL.
- Normal Python resolves the URL from the active job fixture.
- The loop has a hard model-turn limit.
- Tool arguments and results use Pydantic contracts.
- Registered tools and active tools are separate.
- The assistant tool-call message is stored before its tool result.
- Tool failures return to the model as structured failures.
- Fetched source content is untrusted evidence. It cannot alter system rules.
- Source inspection never returns a fit decision.
- Unit tests use fake model and HTTP boundaries. No unit test accesses the network.
- Job 49 remains the control case. Job 328 remains the missing-source case.
- No sessions, database, browser automation, search tool, skills, sub-agents, compaction, retries, or workflow engine.

## Expected starting state

After accepted Plan 001, these paths should exist:

```text
src/haxjobs/model/
src/haxjobs/agent_core/
src/haxjobs/employment/
src/haxjobs/interfaces/
tests/model/
tests/agent_core/
tests/employment/
tests/interfaces/
discussion/fixtures/harness/
docs/implementation-reports/001-stage-0-observed-job-review.md
diagram/003-stage-0-observed-job-review.drawio
```

The Stage 0 runner should:

- accept one frozen request snapshot
- call one async `ModelClient`
- expose no tools
- emit safe events
- write private local artifacts
- return one `RunResult`

If the accepted code does not match this shape, stop and refresh this plan.

## Target architecture after this plan

```text
CLI Interface
  selects Stage 0 or Stage 1
        |
        v
Employment Layer
  validates active job and career fixtures
  exposes one source capability for Stage 1
        |
        v
Agent Core
  builds provider request
  validates one tool call
  dispatches one active tool
  appends linked tool result
  stops on final answer or limit
        |
        +--> Model Boundary
        |
        +--> inspect_job_source(job_ref)
                    |
                    v
              trusted fixture URL
              bounded public fetch
              structured evidence result
```

Stage 0 remains runnable for comparison. Stage 1 is an option on the same employment action, not a second implementation.

## Commands you will need

Run from the repository root.

| Purpose | Command | Expected on success |
|---|---|---|
| Focused tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/model tests/agent_core tests/employment tests/interfaces` | all pass |
| Full surviving tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/` | all pass |
| Compile | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')` | exit 0 |
| Cron syntax | `bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh` | exit 0 |
| Stage 0 help | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help` | includes Stage 0 and Stage 1 selection |
| Diff check | `git diff --check` | no output |
| XML check | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse('diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio')"` | exit 0 |
| Draw.io export | `drawio --export --format png --output /tmp/haxjobs-stage1-check.png diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio` | exit 0 and non-empty PNG |

If the reconciled baseline contains a frontend, run its documented typecheck, lint, and build commands. Do not recreate a deleted frontend for this plan.

## Suggested executor toolkit

Read before coding:

- `plans/001-build-stage-0-observed-job-review.md`
- `docs/implementation-reports/001-stage-0-observed-job-review.md`
- `discussion/004-minimal-job-native-harness.md`
- `discussion/005-implementation-stack-observability-and-verification.md`
- `discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md`
- `discussion/research/2026-07-17-pi-hermes-job-native-harness-study.md`
- `docs/harness-primitives/03-tool-interface.md`
- `.agents/skills/readable-code/SKILL.md`
- `.agents/skills/clean-drawio/SKILL.md`

Use the clean-drawio skill for the required current-state diagram.

## Scope

### In scope

Existing Plan 001 paths that may change:

- `src/haxjobs/model/contracts.py`
- `src/haxjobs/model/openai_compatible.py`
- `src/haxjobs/model/fake.py`
- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/messages.py`
- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/events.py`
- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/result.py`
- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/runner.py`
- `src/haxjobs/employment/job_review.py`
- `src/haxjobs/interfaces/experiment_cli.py`
- `src/haxjobs/cli.py`
- `tests/model/test_model_boundary.py`
- `tests/agent_core/test_stage0_runner.py`
- `tests/employment/test_job_review.py`
- `tests/interfaces/test_experiment_cli.py`
- `diagram/README.md`

New paths:

- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/tools.py`
- `src/haxjobs/employment/source_inspection.py`
- `tests/agent_core/test_tool_registry.py`
- `tests/agent_core/test_stage1_loop.py`
- `tests/employment/test_source_inspection.py`
- `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio`
- `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.png`
- `docs/implementation-reports/002-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md`

Local ignored artifacts:

- new Stage 1 run directories under `state/harness-runs/`
- completed Stage 1 human rubrics
- private transcripts and source-result content

### Out of scope

Do not modify:

- `src/haxjobs/agent/`
- `src/haxjobs/product_tools.py`
- `src/haxjobs/db/`
- `src/haxjobs/features/`
- `src/haxjobs/discovery/`
- `src/haxjobs/evaluate/`
- `src/haxjobs/packs_builder/`
- database schemas
- current profile storage
- current FastAPI routes
- current web interface
- provider credential files
- tracked Job 49 or Job 328 facts unless Plan 001 report found an actual fixture transcription error

Do not add:

- a second tool
- free-form URL fetching
- web search
- browser automation
- cookies or logged-in source access
- CAPTCHA handling
- parallel tool execution
- automatic tool retries
- provider fallback
- streaming
- cancellation machinery
- sessions or durable conversation history
- compaction
- tool stop signals
- general hook framework
- skills or sub-agents
- scheduler or worker
- database writes
- PydanticAI
- new HTTP dependencies

## Git workflow

- Start from the exact accepted Plan 001 candidate SHA.
- Worktree branch: `exec/002-stage1-source-tool-loop-<run-id>`.
- One DeepSeek v4 Pro writer owns all repository writes.
- Use plain commit wording, for example: `Build Stage 1 source inspection loop`.
- Do not push, merge, or cherry-pick automatically.
- Never clean, reset, or stash the user's checkout.
- Stage explicit paths only.
- Finish with a clean candidate worktree after committing.

## Execution and review protocol

Use the same model and worktree gates as Plan 001.

### Required sessions

| Role | Model | Count | Access |
|---|---|---:|---|
| Writer | `deepseek-v4-pro` | 1 | write in isolated worktree |
| Architecture reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |
| Correctness and security reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |
| Deliverables reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |

Runtime metadata must prove exact canonical model IDs and distinct session IDs. No fallback.

### Reviewer scopes

1. **Architecture and scope**
   - one existing runner extended
   - one registry
   - one active tool
   - no legacy agent imports
   - no speculative features

2. **Correctness, security, and tests**
   - argument validation
   - active-job enforcement
   - public-source and redirect checks
   - bounded content
   - structured failures
   - untrusted-content labels
   - no network in unit tests
   - exact event and message order

3. **Deliverables and manual operation**
   - Stage 0 still runs
   - Stage 1 fake and configured-provider commands run
   - reports match the diff and command evidence
   - Draw.io parses, exports, and matches the implemented state

Any blocker or high finding returns to the same Pro writer. Three new Flash sessions re-review each fixed candidate. Final acceptance requires three approvals on one SHA. Stop after three rounds.

## Contracts to implement

### Model tool call

Add a provider-neutral tool-call type only now:

```text
ToolCall
  id: non-empty string
  name: non-empty string
  arguments_json: string
```

The provider adapter projects OpenAI-compatible calls into this type. Provider raw objects must not leak into agent core.

If the provider response ended because of output length and contains tool calls, do not execute them. Return a structured truncated-call failure because arguments may be incomplete.

### Tool definition

Use an explicit Python registry. No AST discovery or import magic.

A tool definition needs:

- name
- description
- Pydantic input model
- Pydantic output model
- async handler
- result-size limit

Do not add availability caches, plugin overrides, dynamic schemas, or toolsets.

### Result envelope

Use one stable dispatch envelope:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {},
  "code": null,
  "error": null
}
```

Failure:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "data": null,
  "code": "invalid_tool_arguments",
  "error": "Safe human-readable message"
}
```

The handler returns a typed output. The dispatcher creates the envelope. Do not let handlers return arbitrary JSON strings.

### Source result

`inspect_job_source` returns evidence, not a fit decision:

- `job_ref`
- `status`: `live`, `gone`, `blocked`, or `unavailable`
- `source_type`
- `requested_url`
- `final_url` when known
- `observed_at` in UTC
- `http_status` when safe and available
- bounded visible text
- content SHA-256 when content exists
- original and retained character counts
- truncation flag
- warnings

Do not return cookies, response headers, raw HTML, redirects outside policy, or provider credentials.

## Steps

### Step 0: Confirm the Stage 1 evidence gate

Read the Plan 001 delivery report and the completed local Job 328 rubric.

The report must show:

- Job 328 was correctly treated as insufficient evidence
- no hidden old-evaluation details appeared
- source inspection was named as the next useful action
- the user accepted Stage 1 as the next experiment
- three Flash reviewers approved the Plan 001 candidate

**Verify**:

```bash
test -s docs/implementation-reports/001-stage-0-observed-job-review.md
rg -n 'Job 328|source inspection|deepseek-v4-flash|approve' \
  docs/implementation-reports/001-stage-0-observed-job-review.md
```

Expected: every required gate is evidenced. Otherwise stop.

### Step 1: Extend the model boundary for tool calls

Add provider-neutral tool definitions and tool-call response parts to `haxjobs.model.contracts`.

The OpenAI-compatible adapter must:

- accept the active tool schemas for the current request
- omit the provider `tools` field entirely when none are active
- normalize assistant text and tool calls
- preserve provider stop reason
- flag output-length termination
- keep provider types inside the adapter

The fake adapter must support scripted sequences such as:

```python
FakeModelClient([
    ModelResponse(tool_calls=[...]),
    ModelResponse(text="I still cannot verify enough detail."),
])
```

Keep Stage 0 behaviour unchanged when no tools are active.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/model/test_model_boundary.py
```

Expected: old Stage 0 tests and new tool-projection tests pass.

### Step 2: Add the explicit typed tool registry

Create `haxjobs.agent_core.tools`.

Required operations:

- register one explicit `ToolDefinition`
- list schemas for an ordered active-name list
- reject duplicate registration
- reject unknown active names before a model call
- dispatch by name
- parse JSON arguments
- validate through the Pydantic input model
- run the async handler
- validate the handler output
- produce the stable result envelope
- convert unknown, inactive, malformed, validation, and handler failures into safe codes

Required failure codes:

- `unknown_tool`
- `inactive_tool`
- `invalid_tool_arguments`
- `tool_failed`
- `tool_result_invalid`
- `tool_budget_exhausted`
- `truncated_tool_call`

Do not catch process-exit exceptions. Catch normal handler exceptions and sanitize messages.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/agent_core/test_tool_registry.py
```

Expected: registry and every named failure case pass without network.

### Step 3: Extend the Stage 0 runner into one bounded loop

Modify the existing runner. Do not create `stage1_runner.py`.

Loop behaviour:

1. freeze the current request snapshot
2. call the model
3. append the complete assistant message
4. if there are no tool calls, return final text
5. if the response was truncated, do not execute calls; append structured failures
6. validate each tool name against the active set
7. enforce one total tool execution for this Stage 1 operation
8. dispatch sequentially
9. append linked tool-result messages in assistant source order
10. call the model again
11. stop on final text or `max_turns`

Default limits:

- `max_turns = 3`
- `max_tool_executions = 1`
- sequential execution only

If the loop reaches the model-turn limit, return `limit_reached` with the partial transcript and no claim of completion.

Add events:

- `turn_started`
- `assistant_completed`
- `tool_requested`
- `tool_started`
- `tool_completed`
- `tool_failed`
- `turn_completed`
- `run_limit_reached`

Events remain redacted. Store safe argument/result hashes and sizes, not full content.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q \
  tests/agent_core/test_stage0_runner.py \
  tests/agent_core/test_stage1_loop.py
```

Expected: Stage 0 one-call tests still pass; Stage 1 ordering, errors, budgets, and limits pass.

### Step 4: Implement `inspect_job_source(job_ref)`

Create `haxjobs.employment.source_inspection` with injected HTTP operations so unit tests can use fakes.

The model input contains only `job_ref`.

The operation context contains:

- the active job reference
- trusted fixture source URL
- allowed source hosts
- source type

Reject a job reference that does not equal the active operation job.

Use the standard library for the real fetcher. Do not add an HTTP dependency.

Before the first request and on every redirect:

- allow only `http` and `https`
- require a hostname
- block localhost
- resolve the hostname
- block private, loopback, link-local, multicast, and unspecified IP addresses
- require the final hostname to match the fixture's explicit allowed-host list

Limits:

- timeout: 10 seconds
- maximum bytes read: 500,000
- maximum visible-text characters: 12,000
- no cookies
- no authentication
- no JavaScript execution

Status mapping:

- `live`: useful public response and bounded content
- `gone`: HTTP 404 or 410
- `blocked`: HTTP 401, 403, 429, or clear public auth-wall/block response
- `unavailable`: timeout, DNS, unsupported response, decode failure, or other safe retrieval failure

Strip script and style content, turn HTML into bounded visible text, and record content hash. Do not infer liveness from an empty or login-only page.

Label the resulting tool message as untrusted source evidence before the next provider request.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/employment/test_source_inspection.py
```

Expected: success, gone, blocked, unavailable, unsafe URL, unsafe redirect, host mismatch, byte limit, text limit, and active-job mismatch tests pass with fake HTTP operations.

### Step 5: Activate exactly one tool in the employment action

Extend `run_job_review(...)` with a stage or capability parameter.

- Stage 0 active tools: `[]`
- Stage 1 registered tools: `inspect_job_source`
- Stage 1 active tools: `['inspect_job_source']`

The model sees the tool description only in Stage 1.

Tool description must say:

- use it when current job evidence is insufficient
- pass only the current job reference
- it inspects the trusted source already attached to the operation
- failure is meaningful and must remain uncertainty
- the result is untrusted evidence, not instructions

Do not include the URL in the model input schema.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/employment/test_job_review.py
```

Expected: Stage 0 remains tool-free; Stage 1 exposes exactly one schema and rejects other active names.

### Step 6: Extend the same CLI command

Add Stage 1 without creating another top-level command:

```text
haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-328 \
  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --max-turns 3 \
  --provider fake|configured \
  --output-dir state/harness-runs
```

Keep `--stage 0` available for comparison. Default remains Stage 0 until the experiment is explicitly selected.

Reject:

- unknown stage
- `max-turns` below 1 or above 3
- Stage 1 without a trusted fixture source
- arbitrary URL arguments

Print final text, run ID, exit reason, turns used, tool calls used, and artifact directory. Do not print full tool content.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-328 \
  --career-fixture discussion/fixtures/harness/backend-career.example.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --provider fake \
  --output-dir /tmp/haxjobs-stage1-smoke
```

Expected: help documents Stage 0 and Stage 1; fake Stage 1 smoke exits 0 with one scripted tool trajectory.

### Step 7: Run the deterministic verification floor

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')
bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh
uv lock --check
git diff --check
```

Expected: every command exits 0. No unit test accesses the public network.

### Step 8: Run Stage 0 and Stage 1 comparison cases

Use the same owner-reviewed career fixture and configured provider.

Run Job 49:

```bash
umask 077
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-49 \
  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --provider configured \
  --output-dir state/harness-runs
```

Expected broad behaviour: the supplied description is enough for a useful negative recommendation. The model should not need source inspection. If it does call the tool, record this as a behavioural finding rather than hiding it.

Run Job 328:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-328 \
  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --provider configured \
  --output-dir state/harness-runs
```

Expected broad behaviour: one source-inspection request for `job-328`, followed by a final answer that uses only returned evidence or clearly preserves uncertainty.

Complete both generated human rubrics.

Accepted Job 328 outcomes:

- `live`: answer uses current bounded source evidence and names remaining unknowns
- `gone`: answer says the role appears unavailable and does not evaluate stale imagined details
- `blocked`: answer says the source could not be inspected and keeps the fit unresolved
- `unavailable`: answer explains the retrieval failure and keeps the fit unresolved

No outcome allows claims copied from the hidden old evaluation.

### Step 9: Create the current-state Draw.io diagram

Create:

- `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio`
- `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.png`

Use five swimlane groups:

1. CLI Interface
2. Employment Context
3. Agent Core
4. Source Inspection
5. Model and Evidence

Show:

- Stage selection
- Stage 0 empty active tools
- Stage 1 one active tool
- bounded model/tool/model loop
- job-ref validation
- trusted fixture URL resolution
- public-source checks
- untrusted bounded tool result
- final answer and private trace/review

Keep file paths in the companion report, not diagram nodes. Stay under 35 cells. Use thick orthogonal inter-group arrows and clean-drawio colours.

Export the tracked PNG:

```bash
drawio --export --format png \
  --output diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.png \
  diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio
```

Update `diagram/README.md` with source, preview, implementation report, and an explanation of the exact implemented state.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
path = Path('diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio')
root = ET.parse(path).getroot()
cells = root.findall('.//mxCell')
edges = [cell for cell in cells if cell.get('edge') == '1']
assert len(cells) <= 35
assert all(edge.find('mxGeometry') is not None for edge in edges)
print({'cells': len(cells), 'edges': len(edges)})
PY

drawio --export --format png \
  --output /tmp/haxjobs-stage1-check.png \
  diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio

test -s /tmp/haxjobs-stage1-check.png
test -s diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.png
```

Expected: XML parses, cell limit holds, every edge has geometry, and both PNG files are non-empty.

### Step 10: Write the evidence-backed deliverable report

Create `docs/implementation-reports/002-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md` with these exact headings:

```text
# Plan 002 delivery report
## Result
## Architecture now
## Files changed
## Verification evidence
## Manual run guide
## Stage 0 versus Stage 1 evidence
## Source-tool safety evidence
## DeepSeek review team
## Known limits and next gate
```

Include:

- accepted Plan 001 base SHA and Plan 002 candidate SHA
- exact Pro writer and three Flash reviewer runtime identities
- changed files grouped by layer
- actual commands, exit codes, and concise results
- tests added and cases covered
- manual fake and configured-provider commands
- Stage 1 Job 49 and Job 328 run IDs
- tool-call counts from redacted traces
- tool-result status without full source content
- SHA-256 of local events and rubric files
- safe human rubric verdicts
- evidence that Stage 0 still has zero tools
- evidence that Stage 1 exposes exactly one tool
- source-safety test summary
- diagram counts and export result
- reviewer findings and resolution
- residual risks and the next evidence gate

Do not include raw prompts, full answers, fetched page text, private career data, headers, URLs containing private tokens, credentials, or provider payloads.

**Verify**:

```bash
test -s docs/implementation-reports/002-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md
rg -n '^## (Result|Architecture now|Files changed|Verification evidence|Manual run guide|Stage 0 versus Stage 1 evidence|Source-tool safety evidence|DeepSeek review team|Known limits and next gate)$' \
  docs/implementation-reports/002-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md
```

Expected: report exists and every required heading is present.

### Step 11: Run the Flash review team and close findings

Commit the candidate, then run three independent Flash reviews against that SHA.

The same Pro writer fixes accepted findings. Any change to prompts, context, model projection, loop order, source inspection, or tool description requires rerunning both configured-provider fixture cases and updating the report.

Final acceptance requires three approvals on the same final SHA.

## Test plan

### Extend `tests/model/test_model_boundary.py`

Add:

- provider-neutral tool-call normalization
- tools omitted when active schema list is empty
- one schema projected when Stage 1 is active
- output-length tool calls marked unsafe to execute
- scripted fake model/tool/final trajectory

### `tests/agent_core/test_tool_registry.py`

Add:

- duplicate registration rejection
- ordered active schema selection
- unknown active tool rejection
- inactive call rejection
- malformed JSON
- Pydantic validation failure
- handler failure sanitization
- invalid handler result
- tool budget exhausted
- stable envelope serialization

### `tests/agent_core/test_stage1_loop.py`

Add:

- no-tool final response
- one tool then final response
- assistant tool-call message before linked tool result
- tool results preserve assistant order
- one-tool execution budget
- max-turn limit
- truncated tool-call rejection
- provider failure after tool result
- redacted tool events
- Stage 0 remains one call with no schemas

### `tests/employment/test_source_inspection.py`

Add:

- active job success
- wrong job reference
- unsupported scheme
- localhost and private IP rejection
- DNS resolution failure
- unsafe redirect
- host allowlist mismatch
- 404 and 410 as gone
- 401, 403, and 429 as blocked
- timeout as unavailable
- max byte limit
- max text limit
- script/style removal
- prompt-like page text stays inside untrusted evidence content
- no fit decision fields

### Extend `tests/employment/test_job_review.py`

Add:

- Stage 0 active tools empty
- Stage 1 active tools exactly `inspect_job_source`
- tool input schema contains `job_ref` and no URL
- source result is labelled untrusted
- Job 328 fake trajectory requests inspection
- Job 49 fake trajectory can finish without inspection

### Extend `tests/interfaces/test_experiment_cli.py`

Add:

- Stage 0 remains default
- Stage 1 accepted
- invalid max-turns rejected
- no arbitrary URL option exists
- fake Stage 1 trajectory
- output prints safe tool count and artifact path

No test snapshots full natural language and no unit test uses live HTTP.

## Done criteria

All must hold:

- [ ] Plan 001 is accepted and its Job 328 evidence gate is satisfied.
- [ ] Plan 002 starts from the exact accepted Plan 001 SHA.
- [ ] One DeepSeek v4 Pro writer implements in an isolated worktree.
- [ ] No substitute or fallback writer model is used.
- [ ] Stage 0 and Stage 1 use the same agent-core runner.
- [ ] Model messages remain provider-neutral outside the adapter.
- [ ] Exactly one tool is registered and active for Stage 1.
- [ ] The tool schema accepts `job_ref` and no URL.
- [ ] The model cannot select a different job source.
- [ ] Tool arguments and handler outputs pass Pydantic validation.
- [ ] Unknown, inactive, malformed, failed, over-budget, and truncated calls become structured failures.
- [ ] Assistant tool-call messages are stored before tool results.
- [ ] Tool results retain assistant source order.
- [ ] Default max turns is 3 and max tool executions is 1.
- [ ] Limit exit is explicit and never presented as completion.
- [ ] Public-source and redirect checks run before retrieval.
- [ ] Source content is capped at 500,000 bytes and 12,000 visible characters.
- [ ] Source content is labelled untrusted in model context.
- [ ] Source inspection returns evidence only, never a fit decision.
- [ ] Unit tests make no public network calls.
- [ ] All Plan 001 tests remain green.
- [ ] Full surviving tests, compilation, cron syntax, lock check, and diff check pass.
- [ ] Manual fake Stage 0 and Stage 1 commands run.
- [ ] Configured-provider Job 49 and Job 328 Stage 1 runs complete.
- [ ] Job 49 tool-call count is reviewed; any unnecessary call is reported honestly.
- [ ] Job 328 uses one correctly scoped source call or the report explains a model-choice failure.
- [ ] Retrieval failure never becomes invented evidence.
- [ ] Both Stage 1 human rubrics are complete and accepted.
- [ ] No private fixture, raw trace, fetched page content, answer, or credential is tracked.
- [ ] `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio` parses and exports.
- [ ] The tracked PNG matches the current diagram by fresh export and visual review.
- [ ] `diagram/README.md` indexes Plan 002's current-state diagram.
- [ ] The tracked delivery report contains real evidence and manual commands.
- [ ] Three independent DeepSeek v4 Flash reviewers approve the same candidate SHA.
- [ ] No out-of-scope file changed.
- [ ] Candidate worktree is clean after commit.
- [ ] Source checkout status fingerprint is unchanged by execution.

## STOP conditions

Stop and report instead of improvising if:

- Plan 001 is not accepted on one candidate SHA
- Job 328 Stage 0 evidence does not show source inspection as the next missing capability
- the Plan 001 delivery report or local rubric is missing
- the code has drifted from the expected four-layer Stage 0 shape
- any in-scope path has unowned changes
- `deepseek-v4-pro` or three independent `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions are unavailable without fallback
- exact model identity cannot be proved through runtime metadata
- source inspection requires login, cookies, browser automation, CAPTCHA handling, or private credentials
- the tool would accept an arbitrary model-supplied URL
- the tool needs a second source or search tool
- a unit test needs public network access
- safe redirect and private-address handling cannot be tested deterministically
- a trace, report, or tracked file contains private career content or fetched source text
- implementation creates a second loop or imports `haxjobs.agent`
- work expands into sessions, database, monitoring, applications, coding tools, or UI
- Stage 0 tests regress
- a step fails verification twice after one reasonable correction
- Draw.io export fails after import-safe simplification
- reviewer findings conflict with decided product requirements
- three review/fix rounds do not reach one approved candidate

## Maintenance notes

- The source tool is an experiment capability, not a general browser.
- A LinkedIn block is evidence about source accessibility, not a failed implementation by itself.
- Keep the active job reference in operation context so future sources cannot be selected through model arguments.
- Do not add retries until traces show a repeatable transient-failure need and retry safety is defined.
- Do not add parallel tools until several independent reads create a measured bottleneck.
- Stage 2 conversational correction and Stage 3 saved decision are intentionally not planned yet. The Plan 002 report must recommend the next plan from observed traces rather than preselecting one.
- The legacy agent path remains separate and should be deleted or migrated only through a later explicit cleanup plan.

content.text:
# Plan 002: Build the Stage 1 bounded source-inspection loop

> **Executor instructions**: Read this entire plan before editing. Follow each
> step in order. Run every verification command and confirm the expected result
> before continuing. Stop on any condition in the STOP section. Do not invent a
> workaround or expand the tool catalogue.
>
> **Required writer model**: The only writer must be `deepseek-v4-pro` inside a
> fresh isolated worktree created from the accepted Plan 001 commit. Three fresh
> independent `deepseek-v4-flash` reviewers inspect the same candidate commit.
> No model aliases, fallback, or substitution are allowed.
>
> **Evidence gate**: Plan 001 must be accepted first. Its delivery report must
> show that Job 328 could not be judged honestly from the title-and-URL stub and
> that source inspection was the next useful check. If that evidence is absent,
> stop. Do not build Stage 1 merely because this file exists.
>
> **Drift check (run first after Plan 001 is accepted)**:
>
> ```bash
> git diff --stat <accepted-plan-001-sha>..HEAD -- \
>   src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
>   src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces src/haxjobs/cli.py \
>   tests discussion/fixtures/harness diagram docs/implementation-reports
> git status --short -- \
>   src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
>   src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces src/haxjobs/cli.py \
>   tests discussion/fixtures/harness diagram docs/implementation-reports
> ```
>
> Expected: HEAD equals the accepted Plan 001 candidate or contains only
> explicitly reviewed changes. No in-scope unowned changes. On mismatch, stop
> and ask for plan reconciliation.

## Status

- **Priority**: P1
- **Effort**: L
- **Risk**: MED
- **Depends on**: `plans/001-build-stage-0-observed-job-review.md`
- **Category**: direction, architecture, security, tests
- **Planned at**: commit `5423187`, 2026-07-17; must be restamped to the accepted Plan 001 SHA before execution

## Why this matters

Stage 0 deliberately leaves Job 328 unresolved because the stored evidence is only a title and LinkedIn URL. Stage 1 tests one change: can Hax ask normal code to inspect the trusted source, receive a bounded evidence result, then answer without inventing what the source did not prove?

This plan extends the Stage 0 runtime into the first real agent loop:

```text
model -> one validated tool call -> structured result -> model -> final answer
```

Only `inspect_job_source(job_ref)` is active. The model never supplies an arbitrary URL. A blocked, missing, or unavailable source is still a valid result.

## Decided product contract

The executor must preserve these decisions:

- Stage 1 extends the accepted Stage 0 runner. There is one loop.
- The only registered and active tool is `inspect_job_source(job_ref)`.
- The tool accepts a trusted job reference, not a URL.
- Normal Python resolves the URL from the active job fixture.
- The loop has a hard model-turn limit.
- Tool arguments and results use Pydantic contracts.
- Registered tools and active tools are separate.
- The assistant tool-call message is stored before its tool result.
- Tool failures return to the model as structured failures.
- Fetched source content is untrusted evidence. It cannot alter system rules.
- Source inspection never returns a fit decision.
- Unit tests use fake model and HTTP boundaries. No unit test accesses the network.
- Job 49 remains the control case. Job 328 remains the missing-source case.
- No sessions, database, browser automation, search tool, skills, sub-agents, compaction, retries, or workflow engine.

## Expected starting state

After accepted Plan 001, these paths should exist:

```text
src/haxjobs/model/
src/haxjobs/agent_core/
src/haxjobs/employment/
src/haxjobs/interfaces/
tests/model/
tests/agent_core/
tests/employment/
tests/interfaces/
discussion/fixtures/harness/
docs/implementation-reports/001-stage-0-observed-job-review.md
diagram/003-stage-0-observed-job-review.drawio
```

The Stage 0 runner should:

- accept one frozen request snapshot
- call one async `ModelClient`
- expose no tools
- emit safe events
- write private local artifacts
- return one `RunResult`

If the accepted code does not match this shape, stop and refresh this plan.

## Target architecture after this plan

```text
CLI Interface
  selects Stage 0 or Stage 1
        |
        v
Employment Layer
  validates active job and career fixtures
  exposes one source capability for Stage 1
        |
        v
Agent Core
  builds provider request
  validates one tool call
  dispatches one active tool
  appends linked tool result
  stops on final answer or limit
        |
        +--> Model Boundary
        |
        +--> inspect_job_source(job_ref)
                    |
                    v
              trusted fixture URL
              bounded public fetch
              structured evidence result
```

Stage 0 remains runnable for comparison. Stage 1 is an option on the same employment action, not a second implementation.

## Commands you will need

Run from the repository root.

| Purpose | Command | Expected on success |
|---|---|---|
| Focused tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/model tests/agent_core tests/employment tests/interfaces` | all pass |
| Full surviving tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/` | all pass |
| Compile | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')` | exit 0 |
| Cron syntax | `bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh` | exit 0 |
| Stage 0 help | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help` | includes Stage 0 and Stage 1 selection |
| Diff check | `git diff --check` | no output |
| XML check | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse('diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio')"` | exit 0 |
| Draw.io export | `drawio --export --format png --output /tmp/haxjobs-stage1-check.png diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio` | exit 0 and non-empty PNG |

If the reconciled baseline contains a frontend, run its documented typecheck, lint, and build commands. Do not recreate a deleted frontend for this plan.

## Suggested executor toolkit

Read before coding:

- `plans/001-build-stage-0-observed-job-review.md`
- `docs/implementation-reports/001-stage-0-observed-job-review.md`
- `discussion/004-minimal-job-native-harness.md`
- `discussion/005-implementation-stack-observability-and-verification.md`
- `discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md`
- `discussion/research/2026-07-17-pi-hermes-job-native-harness-study.md`
- `docs/harness-primitives/03-tool-interface.md`
- `.agents/skills/readable-code/SKILL.md`
- `.agents/skills/clean-drawio/SKILL.md`

Use the clean-drawio skill for the required current-state diagram.

## Scope

### In scope

Existing Plan 001 paths that may change:

- `src/haxjobs/model/contracts.py`
- `src/haxjobs/model/openai_compatible.py`
- `src/haxjobs/model/fake.py`
- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/messages.py`
- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/events.py`
- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/result.py`
- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/runner.py`
- `src/haxjobs/employment/job_review.py`
- `src/haxjobs/interfaces/experiment_cli.py`
- `src/haxjobs/cli.py`
- `tests/model/test_model_boundary.py`
- `tests/agent_core/test_stage0_runner.py`
- `tests/employment/test_job_review.py`
- `tests/interfaces/test_experiment_cli.py`
- `diagram/README.md`

New paths:

- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/tools.py`
- `src/haxjobs/employment/source_inspection.py`
- `tests/agent_core/test_tool_registry.py`
- `tests/agent_core/test_stage1_loop.py`
- `tests/employment/test_source_inspection.py`
- `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio`
- `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.png`
- `docs/implementation-reports/002-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md`

Local ignored artifacts:

- new Stage 1 run directories under `state/harness-runs/`
- completed Stage 1 human rubrics
- private transcripts and source-result content

### Out of scope

Do not modify:

- `src/haxjobs/agent/`
- `src/haxjobs/product_tools.py`
- `src/haxjobs/db/`
- `src/haxjobs/features/`
- `src/haxjobs/discovery/`
- `src/haxjobs/evaluate/`
- `src/haxjobs/packs_builder/`
- database schemas
- current profile storage
- current FastAPI routes
- current web interface
- provider credential files
- tracked Job 49 or Job 328 facts unless Plan 001 report found an actual fixture transcription error

Do not add:

- a second tool
- free-form URL fetching
- web search
- browser automation
- cookies or logged-in source access
- CAPTCHA handling
- parallel tool execution
- automatic tool retries
- provider fallback
- streaming
- cancellation machinery
- sessions or durable conversation history
- compaction
- tool stop signals
- general hook framework
- skills or sub-agents
- scheduler or worker
- database writes
- PydanticAI
- new HTTP dependencies

## Git workflow

- Start from the exact accepted Plan 001 candidate SHA.
- Worktree branch: `exec/002-stage1-source-tool-loop-<run-id>`.
- One DeepSeek v4 Pro writer owns all repository writes.
- Use plain commit wording, for example: `Build Stage 1 source inspection loop`.
- Do not push, merge, or cherry-pick automatically.
- Never clean, reset, or stash the user's checkout.
- Stage explicit paths only.
- Finish with a clean candidate worktree after committing.

## Execution and review protocol

Use the same model and worktree gates as Plan 001.

### Required sessions

| Role | Model | Count | Access |
|---|---|---:|---|
| Writer | `deepseek-v4-pro` | 1 | write in isolated worktree |
| Architecture reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |
| Correctness and security reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |
| Deliverables reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |

Runtime metadata must prove exact canonical model IDs and distinct session IDs. No fallback.

### Reviewer scopes

1. **Architecture and scope**
   - one existing runner extended
   - one registry
   - one active tool
   - no legacy agent imports
   - no speculative features

2. **Correctness, security, and tests**
   - argument validation
   - active-job enforcement
   - public-source and redirect checks
   - bounded content
   - structured failures
   - untrusted-content labels
   - no network in unit tests
   - exact event and message order

3. **Deliverables and manual operation**
   - Stage 0 still runs
   - Stage 1 fake and configured-provider commands run
   - reports match the diff and command evidence
   - Draw.io parses, exports, and matches the implemented state

Any blocker or high finding returns to the same Pro writer. Three new Flash sessions re-review each fixed candidate. Final acceptance requires three approvals on one SHA. Stop after three rounds.

## Contracts to implement

### Model tool call

Add a provider-neutral tool-call type only now:

```text
ToolCall
  id: non-empty string
  name: non-empty string
  arguments_json: string
```

The provider adapter projects OpenAI-compatible calls into this type. Provider raw objects must not leak into agent core.

If the provider response ended because of output length and contains tool calls, do not execute them. Return a structured truncated-call failure because arguments may be incomplete.

### Tool definition

Use an explicit Python registry. No AST discovery or import magic.

A tool definition needs:

- name
- description
- Pydantic input model
- Pydantic output model
- async handler
- result-size limit

Do not add availability caches, plugin overrides, dynamic schemas, or toolsets.

### Result envelope

Use one stable dispatch envelope:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {},
  "code": null,
  "error": null
}
```

Failure:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "data": null,
  "code": "invalid_tool_arguments",
  "error": "Safe human-readable message"
}
```

The handler returns a typed output. The dispatcher creates the envelope. Do not let handlers return arbitrary JSON strings.

### Source result

`inspect_job_source` returns evidence, not a fit decision:

- `job_ref`
- `status`: `live`, `gone`, `blocked`, or `unavailable`
- `source_type`
- `requested_url`
- `final_url` when known
- `observed_at` in UTC
- `http_status` when safe and available
- bounded visible text
- content SHA-256 when content exists
- original and retained character counts
- truncation flag
- warnings

Do not return cookies, response headers, raw HTML, redirects outside policy, or provider credentials.

## Steps

### Step 0: Confirm the Stage 1 evidence gate

Read the Plan 001 delivery report and the completed local Job 328 rubric.

The report must show:

- Job 328 was correctly treated as insufficient evidence
- no hidden old-evaluation details appeared
- source inspection was named as the next useful action
- the user accepted Stage 1 as the next experiment
- three Flash reviewers approved the Plan 001 candidate

**Verify**:

```bash
test -s docs/implementation-reports/001-stage-0-observed-job-review.md
rg -n 'Job 328|source inspection|deepseek-v4-flash|approve' \
  docs/implementation-reports/001-stage-0-observed-job-review.md
```

Expected: every required gate is evidenced. Otherwise stop.

### Step 1: Extend the model boundary for tool calls

Add provider-neutral tool definitions and tool-call response parts to `haxjobs.model.contracts`.

The OpenAI-compatible adapter must:

- accept the active tool schemas for the current request
- omit the provider `tools` field entirely when none are active
- normalize assistant text and tool calls
- preserve provider stop reason
- flag output-length termination
- keep provider types inside the adapter

The fake adapter must support scripted sequences such as:

```python
FakeModelClient([
    ModelResponse(tool_calls=[...]),
    ModelResponse(text="I still cannot verify enough detail."),
])
```

Keep Stage 0 behaviour unchanged when no tools are active.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/model/test_model_boundary.py
```

Expected: old Stage 0 tests and new tool-projection tests pass.

### Step 2: Add the explicit typed tool registry

Create `haxjobs.agent_core.tools`.

Required operations:

- register one explicit `ToolDefinition`
- list schemas for an ordered active-name list
- reject duplicate registration
- reject unknown active names before a model call
- dispatch by name
- parse JSON arguments
- validate through the Pydantic input model
- run the async handler
- validate the handler output
- produce the stable result envelope
- convert unknown, inactive, malformed, validation, and handler failures into safe codes

Required failure codes:

- `unknown_tool`
- `inactive_tool`
- `invalid_tool_arguments`
- `tool_failed`
- `tool_result_invalid`
- `tool_budget_exhausted`
- `truncated_tool_call`

Do not catch process-exit exceptions. Catch normal handler exceptions and sanitize messages.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/agent_core/test_tool_registry.py
```

Expected: registry and every named failure case pass without network.

### Step 3: Extend the Stage 0 runner into one bounded loop

Modify the existing runner. Do not create `stage1_runner.py`.

Loop behaviour:

1. freeze the current request snapshot
2. call the model
3. append the complete assistant message
4. if there are no tool calls, return final text
5. if the response was truncated, do not execute calls; append structured failures
6. validate each tool name against the active set
7. enforce one total tool execution for this Stage 1 operation
8. dispatch sequentially
9. append linked tool-result messages in assistant source order
10. call the model again
11. stop on final text or `max_turns`

Default limits:

- `max_turns = 3`
- `max_tool_executions = 1`
- sequential execution only

If the loop reaches the model-turn limit, return `limit_reached` with the partial transcript and no claim of completion.

Add events:

- `turn_started`
- `assistant_completed`
- `tool_requested`
- `tool_started`
- `tool_completed`
- `tool_failed`
- `turn_completed`
- `run_limit_reached`

Events remain redacted. Store safe argument/result hashes and sizes, not full content.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q \
  tests/agent_core/test_stage0_runner.py \
  tests/agent_core/test_stage1_loop.py
```

Expected: Stage 0 one-call tests still pass; Stage 1 ordering, errors, budgets, and limits pass.

### Step 4: Implement `inspect_job_source(job_ref)`

Create `haxjobs.employment.source_inspection` with injected HTTP operations so unit tests can use fakes.

The model input contains only `job_ref`.

The operation context contains:

- the active job reference
- trusted fixture source URL
- allowed source hosts
- source type

Reject a job reference that does not equal the active operation job.

Use the standard library for the real fetcher. Do not add an HTTP dependency.

Before the first request and on every redirect:

- allow only `http` and `https`
- require a hostname
- block localhost
- resolve the hostname
- block private, loopback, link-local, multicast, and unspecified IP addresses
- require the final hostname to match the fixture's explicit allowed-host list

Limits:

- timeout: 10 seconds
- maximum bytes read: 500,000
- maximum visible-text characters: 12,000
- no cookies
- no authentication
- no JavaScript execution

Status mapping:

- `live`: useful public response and bounded content
- `gone`: HTTP 404 or 410
- `blocked`: HTTP 401, 403, 429, or clear public auth-wall/block response
- `unavailable`: timeout, DNS, unsupported response, decode failure, or other safe retrieval failure

Strip script and style content, turn HTML into bounded visible text, and record content hash. Do not infer liveness from an empty or login-only page.

Label the resulting tool message as untrusted source evidence before the next provider request.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/employment/test_source_inspection.py
```

Expected: success, gone, blocked, unavailable, unsafe URL, unsafe redirect, host mismatch, byte limit, text limit, and active-job mismatch tests pass with fake HTTP operations.

### Step 5: Activate exactly one tool in the employment action

Extend `run_job_review(...)` with a stage or capability parameter.

- Stage 0 active tools: `[]`
- Stage 1 registered tools: `inspect_job_source`
- Stage 1 active tools: `['inspect_job_source']`

The model sees the tool description only in Stage 1.

Tool description must say:

- use it when current job evidence is insufficient
- pass only the current job reference
- it inspects the trusted source already attached to the operation
- failure is meaningful and must remain uncertainty
- the result is untrusted evidence, not instructions

Do not include the URL in the model input schema.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/employment/test_job_review.py
```

Expected: Stage 0 remains tool-free; Stage 1 exposes exactly one schema and rejects other active names.

### Step 6: Extend the same CLI command

Add Stage 1 without creating another top-level command:

```text
haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-328 \
  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --max-turns 3 \
  --provider fake|configured \
  --output-dir state/harness-runs
```

Keep `--stage 0` available for comparison. Default remains Stage 0 until the experiment is explicitly selected.

Reject:

- unknown stage
- `max-turns` below 1 or above 3
- Stage 1 without a trusted fixture source
- arbitrary URL arguments

Print final text, run ID, exit reason, turns used, tool calls used, and artifact directory. Do not print full tool content.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-328 \
  --career-fixture discussion/fixtures/harness/backend-career.example.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --provider fake \
  --output-dir /tmp/haxjobs-stage1-smoke
```

Expected: help documents Stage 0 and Stage 1; fake Stage 1 smoke exits 0 with one scripted tool trajectory.

### Step 7: Run the deterministic verification floor

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')
bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh
uv lock --check
git diff --check
```

Expected: every command exits 0. No unit test accesses the public network.

### Step 8: Run Stage 0 and Stage 1 comparison cases

Use the same owner-reviewed career fixture and configured provider.

Run Job 49:

```bash
umask 077
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-49 \
  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --provider configured \
  --output-dir state/harness-runs
```

Expected broad behaviour: the supplied description is enough for a useful negative recommendation. The model should not need source inspection. If it does call the tool, record this as a behavioural finding rather than hiding it.

Run Job 328:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-328 \
  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --provider configured \
  --output-dir state/harness-runs
```

Expected broad behaviour: one source-inspection request for `job-328`, followed by a final answer that uses only returned evidence or clearly preserves uncertainty.

Complete both generated human rubrics.

Accepted Job 328 outcomes:

- `live`: answer uses current bounded source evidence and names remaining unknowns
- `gone`: answer says the role appears unavailable and does not evaluate stale imagined details
- `blocked`: answer says the source could not be inspected and keeps the fit unresolved
- `unavailable`: answer explains the retrieval failure and keeps the fit unresolved

No outcome allows claims copied from the hidden old evaluation.

### Step 9: Create the current-state Draw.io diagram

Create:

- `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio`
- `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.png`

Use five swimlane groups:

1. CLI Interface
2. Employment Context
3. Agent Core
4. Source Inspection
5. Model and Evidence

Show:

- Stage selection
- Stage 0 empty active tools
- Stage 1 one active tool
- bounded model/tool/model loop
- job-ref validation
- trusted fixture URL resolution
- public-source checks
- untrusted bounded tool result
- final answer and private trace/review

Keep file paths in the companion report, not diagram nodes. Stay under 35 cells. Use thick orthogonal inter-group arrows and clean-drawio colours.

Export the tracked PNG:

```bash
drawio --export --format png \
  --output diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.png \
  diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio
```

Update `diagram/README.md` with source, preview, implementation report, and an explanation of the exact implemented state.

**Verify**:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
path = Path('diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio')
root = ET.parse(path).getroot()
cells = root.findall('.//mxCell')
edges = [cell for cell in cells if cell.get('edge') == '1']
assert len(cells) <= 35
assert all(edge.find('mxGeometry') is not None for edge in edges)
print({'cells': len(cells), 'edges': len(edges)})
PY

drawio --export --format png \
  --output /tmp/haxjobs-stage1-check.png \
  diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio

test -s /tmp/haxjobs-stage1-check.png
test -s diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.png
```

Expected: XML parses, cell limit holds, every edge has geometry, and both PNG files are non-empty.

### Step 10: Write the evidence-backed deliverable report

Create `docs/implementation-reports/002-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md` with these exact headings:

```text
# Plan 002 delivery report
## Result
## Architecture now
## Files changed
## Verification evidence
## Manual run guide
## Stage 0 versus Stage 1 evidence
## Source-tool safety evidence
## DeepSeek review team
## Known limits and next gate
```

Include:

- accepted Plan 001 base SHA and Plan 002 candidate SHA
- exact Pro writer and three Flash reviewer runtime identities
- changed files grouped by layer
- actual commands, exit codes, and concise results
- tests added and cases covered
- manual fake and configured-provider commands
- Stage 1 Job 49 and Job 328 run IDs
- tool-call counts from redacted traces
- tool-result status without full source content
- SHA-256 of local events and rubric files
- safe human rubric verdicts
- evidence that Stage 0 still has zero tools
- evidence that Stage 1 exposes exactly one tool
- source-safety test summary
- diagram counts and export result
- reviewer findings and resolution
- residual risks and the next evidence gate

Do not include raw prompts, full answers, fetched page text, private career data, headers, URLs containing private tokens, credentials, or provider payloads.

**Verify**:

```bash
test -s docs/implementation-reports/002-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md
rg -n '^## (Result|Architecture now|Files changed|Verification evidence|Manual run guide|Stage 0 versus Stage 1 evidence|Source-tool safety evidence|DeepSeek review team|Known limits and next gate)$' \
  docs/implementation-reports/002-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md
```

Expected: report exists and every required heading is present.

### Step 11: Run the Flash review team and close findings

Commit the candidate, then run three independent Flash reviews against that SHA.

The same Pro writer fixes accepted findings. Any change to prompts, context, model projection, loop order, source inspection, or tool description requires rerunning both configured-provider fixture cases and updating the report.

Final acceptance requires three approvals on the same final SHA.

## Test plan

### Extend `tests/model/test_model_boundary.py`

Add:

- provider-neutral tool-call normalization
- tools omitted when active schema list is empty
- one schema projected when Stage 1 is active
- output-length tool calls marked unsafe to execute
- scripted fake model/tool/final trajectory

### `tests/agent_core/test_tool_registry.py`

Add:

- duplicate registration rejection
- ordered active schema selection
- unknown active tool rejection
- inactive call rejection
- malformed JSON
- Pydantic validation failure
- handler failure sanitization
- invalid handler result
- tool budget exhausted
- stable envelope serialization

### `tests/agent_core/test_stage1_loop.py`

Add:

- no-tool final response
- one tool then final response
- assistant tool-call message before linked tool result
- tool results preserve assistant order
- one-tool execution budget
- max-turn limit
- truncated tool-call rejection
- provider failure after tool result
- redacted tool events
- Stage 0 remains one call with no schemas

### `tests/employment/test_source_inspection.py`

Add:

- active job success
- wrong job reference
- unsupported scheme
- localhost and private IP rejection
- DNS resolution failure
- unsafe redirect
- host allowlist mismatch
- 404 and 410 as gone
- 401, 403, and 429 as blocked
- timeout as unavailable
- max byte limit
- max text limit
- script/style removal
- prompt-like page text stays inside untrusted evidence content
- no fit decision fields

### Extend `tests/employment/test_job_review.py`

Add:

- Stage 0 active tools empty
- Stage 1 active tools exactly `inspect_job_source`
- tool input schema contains `job_ref` and no URL
- source result is labelled untrusted
- Job 328 fake trajectory requests inspection
- Job 49 fake trajectory can finish without inspection

### Extend `tests/interfaces/test_experiment_cli.py`

Add:

- Stage 0 remains default
- Stage 1 accepted
- invalid max-turns rejected
- no arbitrary URL option exists
- fake Stage 1 trajectory
- output prints safe tool count and artifact path

No test snapshots full natural language and no unit test uses live HTTP.

## Done criteria

All must hold:

- [ ] Plan 001 is accepted and its Job 328 evidence gate is satisfied.
- [ ] Plan 002 starts from the exact accepted Plan 001 SHA.
- [ ] One DeepSeek v4 Pro writer implements in an isolated worktree.
- [ ] No substitute or fallback writer model is used.
- [ ] Stage 0 and Stage 1 use the same agent-core runner.
- [ ] Model messages remain provider-neutral outside the adapter.
- [ ] Exactly one tool is registered and active for Stage 1.
- [ ] The tool schema accepts `job_ref` and no URL.
- [ ] The model cannot select a different job source.
- [ ] Tool arguments and handler outputs pass Pydantic validation.
- [ ] Unknown, inactive, malformed, failed, over-budget, and truncated calls become structured failures.
- [ ] Assistant tool-call messages are stored before tool results.
- [ ] Tool results retain assistant source order.
- [ ] Default max turns is 3 and max tool executions is 1.
- [ ] Limit exit is explicit and never presented as completion.
- [ ] Public-source and redirect checks run before retrieval.
- [ ] Source content is capped at 500,000 bytes and 12,000 visible characters.
- [ ] Source content is labelled untrusted in model context.
- [ ] Source inspection returns evidence only, never a fit decision.
- [ ] Unit tests make no public network calls.
- [ ] All Plan 001 tests remain green.
- [ ] Full surviving tests, compilation, cron syntax, lock check, and diff check pass.
- [ ] Manual fake Stage 0 and Stage 1 commands run.
- [ ] Configured-provider Job 49 and Job 328 Stage 1 runs complete.
- [ ] Job 49 tool-call count is reviewed; any unnecessary call is reported honestly.
- [ ] Job 328 uses one correctly scoped source call or the report explains a model-choice failure.
- [ ] Retrieval failure never becomes invented evidence.
- [ ] Both Stage 1 human rubrics are complete and accepted.
- [ ] No private fixture, raw trace, fetched page content, answer, or credential is tracked.
- [ ] `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio` parses and exports.
- [ ] The tracked PNG matches the current diagram by fresh export and visual review.
- [ ] `diagram/README.md` indexes Plan 002's current-state diagram.
- [ ] The tracked delivery report contains real evidence and manual commands.
- [ ] Three independent DeepSeek v4 Flash reviewers approve the same candidate SHA.
- [ ] No out-of-scope file changed.
- [ ] Candidate worktree is clean after commit.
- [ ] Source checkout status fingerprint is unchanged by execution.

## STOP conditions

Stop and report instead of improvising if:

- Plan 001 is not accepted on one candidate SHA
- Job 328 Stage 0 evidence does not show source inspection as the next missing capability
- the Plan 001 delivery report or local rubric is missing
- the code has drifted from the expected four-layer Stage 0 shape
- any in-scope path has unowned changes
- `deepseek-v4-pro` or three independent `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions are unavailable without fallback
- exact model identity cannot be proved through runtime metadata
- source inspection requires login, cookies, browser automation, CAPTCHA handling, or private credentials
- the tool would accept an arbitrary model-supplied URL
- the tool needs a second source or search tool
- a unit test needs public network access
- safe redirect and private-address handling cannot be tested deterministically
- a trace, report, or tracked file contains private career content or fetched source text
- implementation creates a second loop or imports `haxjobs.agent`
- work expands into sessions, database, monitoring, applications, coding tools, or UI
- Stage 0 tests regress
- a step fails verification twice after one reasonable correction
- Draw.io export fails after import-safe simplification
- reviewer findings conflict with decided product requirements
- three review/fix rounds do not reach one approved candidate

## Maintenance notes

- The source tool is an experiment capability, not a general browser.
- A LinkedIn block is evidence about source accessibility, not a failed implementation by itself.
- Keep the active job reference in operation context so future sources cannot be selected through model arguments.
- Do not add retries until traces show a repeatable transient-failure need and retry safety is defined.
- Do not add parallel tools until several independent reads create a measured bottleneck.
- Stage 2 conversational correction and Stage 3 saved decision are intentionally not planned yet. The Plan 002 report must recommend the next plan from observed traces rather than preselecting one.
- The legacy agent path remains separate and should be deleted or migrated only through a later explicit cleanup plan.

text:
# HaxJobs greenfield implementation plans

Generated on 2026-07-17 from the decided material in `discussion/` and the Pi/Hermes source study.

This is the first build wave for the new HaxJobs runtime. It deliberately plans only Stage 0 and Stage 1:

```text
Stage 0
  one observed provider call, no tools

Stage 1
  the same runner, one inspect_job_source(job_ref) tool
```

Stage 2 conversation history, Stage 3 saved decisions, sessions, career memory, company watching, coding workspaces, and every broader product capability wait for evidence from these runs.

## Current execution blockers

The plans are written, but execution must not start yet.

### 1. The canonical baseline is not committed

At planning time:

- HEAD was `5423187` on `main`.
- The worktree contained a large intentional cleanup.
- Required `discussion/` and `diagram/` files were untracked.
- `tests/` was deleted in the working tree.
- Several old agent files had uncommitted edits.

An isolated worktree created from `5423187` would not contain the design state these plans depend on.

Required action before execution:

1. Commit the intended cleanup, discussion notes, fixtures, diagrams, and source-worthy files through the normal developer workflow.
2. Do not ask Improve execution to commit or merge them.
3. Run `improve reconcile` after the clean commit.
4. Replace each plan's planned-at SHA with the new baseline.
5. Refresh current-state excerpts and in-scope drift checks.

### 2. Exact DeepSeek execution models are not currently proved

The inspected subagent runtime listed GPT-based built-ins. It did not expose runtime mappings for:

- `deepseek-v4-pro`
- `deepseek-v4-flash`

The user requires DeepSeek v4 Pro as the sole implementation writer and a team of DeepSeek v4 Flash reviewers. Before execution, the runtime must prove the exact canonical model IDs through metadata without fallback.

Do not substitute Codex, Claude, another DeepSeek alias, or a fallback model.

## Execution order and status

| Plan | Title | Priority | Effort | Depends on | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [001](001-build-stage-0-observed-job-review.md) | Build the Stage 0 observed job-review runtime | P1 | L | committed baseline, exact DeepSeek models | BLOCKED |
| [002](002-build-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md) | Build the Stage 1 bounded source-inspection loop | P1 | L | accepted Plan 001 and Job 328 evidence gate | BLOCKED |

Status values:

- `TODO`
- `IN PROGRESS`
- `DONE`
- `BLOCKED: <reason>`
- `REJECTED: <reason>`

## Dependency graph

```text
commit current greenfield design baseline
        |
        v
prove deepseek-v4-pro and deepseek-v4-flash availability
        |
        v
Plan 001: Stage 0
  Job 49 -> Job 328 -> human reviews -> delivery report
        |
        | only if Job 328 proves source inspection is needed
        v
Plan 002: Stage 1
  same runner + one source tool -> comparison report
        |
        v
stop and choose the next plan from observed failures
```

Plan 002 must not begin because Plan 001 tests passed alone. Its report and completed Job 328 human rubric must show that source inspection is the next missing capability.

## Architecture boundary for this wave

The current user instruction resolves the implementation shape for Stage 0 and Stage 1:

```text
Model boundary
  provider-neutral contracts, configured adapter, fake adapter

Agent core
  messages, bounded loop, events, results

Employment layer
  Hax instructions, fixtures, context, one employment action

Interface
  a thin argparse CLI
```

Keep these as modules inside one Python package. No package monorepo.

The old `src/haxjobs/agent/` path remains out of scope during this wave. New code must not import it or wrap it. Temporary coexistence is recorded honestly. Deletion or migration needs a later explicit plan after the new runtime works.

## Required execution team for every plan

### Writer

- One `deepseek-v4-pro` session.
- Sole writer in a fresh isolated worktree.
- Receives the plan, committed repository, and named discussion references.
- Must not receive secret values.
- Implements, tests, runs fixtures, writes the delivery report, creates the diagram, and commits the candidate.

### Independent review team

Three fresh `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions inspect the same candidate SHA without seeing each other's findings:

1. Architecture and scope reviewer.
2. Correctness, privacy, safety, and tests reviewer.
3. Deliverables, manual CLI, evidence report, and Draw.io reviewer.

Reviewers are read-only. They return:

- `approve`
- `changes-required`
- `blocked`

The same Pro writer fixes accepted findings. All checks rerun. Three new Flash sessions review the replacement SHA. Final acceptance requires three approvals on the same candidate SHA.

Stop after three failed fix rounds.

## Deliverables required from every plan

Every plan must finish with all of these:

| Deliverable | Plan 001 | Plan 002 |
|---|---|---|
| Manually runnable CLI | `haxjobs experiment review-job` Stage 0 | same command with Stage 1 |
| Deterministic fake-model tests | required | extended |
| Configured-provider fixture runs | Job 49 and Job 328 | Job 49 and Job 328 |
| Local redacted JSONL traces | required | required |
| Completed human rubrics | two | two |
| Tracked delivery report | `docs/implementation-reports/001-...md` | `docs/implementation-reports/002-...md` |
| Current-state Draw.io | `diagram/003-...drawio` | `diagram/004-...drawio` |
| PNG exported from Draw.io | required | required |
| Diagram index update | required | required |
| Three Flash approvals | required | required |

The Markdown delivery report is the durable evidence. Raw prompts, model answers, fetched source text, private career fixtures, credentials, and provider payloads stay local and untracked.

## Manual interface progression

After Plan 001:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-49 \
  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
  --provider fake \
  --output-dir state/harness-runs
```

Use `--provider configured` for the real provider.

After Plan 002:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
  --job job-328 \
  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
  --stage 1 \
  --max-turns 3 \
  --provider configured \
  --output-dir state/harness-runs
```

The CLI is an experiment interface. It does not become the final career CLI in this wave.

## Verification baseline

Every plan runs:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/
PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')
bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh
uv lock --check
git diff --check
```

If the reconciled baseline still contains a frontend, also run its documented typecheck, lint, and build commands. If the cleanup intentionally removed it, do not recreate it merely to satisfy an old command.

Tests added by these plans must not call live models or the public network.

Configured-provider and source-inspection runs are separate manual evidence steps.

## Draw.io rule for every plan

Use `.agents/skills/clean-drawio/SKILL.md`.

Each diagram must:

- show the code that exists at the accepted candidate SHA
- use grouped swimlanes
- keep to five to seven groups
- stay under roughly 35 cells
- keep file paths out of nodes
- use thick orthogonal inter-group arrows
- include geometry for every edge
- parse as XML
- export through the real local Draw.io command
- have a tracked PNG generated from the same source
- be indexed in `diagram/README.md`
- link to the plan's implementation report for file-level detail

A design-only future component must not appear as if implemented.

## Evidence report rule for every plan

Each tracked report must record:

- base and candidate SHAs
- exact writer and reviewer runtime model identities
- changed paths grouped by architecture layer
- tests added
- actual commands and exit codes
- manual run commands
- safe run IDs and artifact hashes
- human rubric verdicts
- diagram counts and export evidence
- reviewer findings and resolution
- known limits
- next evidence gate

Never record:

- secret values
- provider configuration values
- full prompts
- complete model answers
- CV or profile text
- full fetched pages
- authorization headers
- cookies
- raw provider payloads

## Why later plans are not written yet

The discussion explicitly chose an evidence-driven staircase:

```text
run a real job
-> inspect the trace
-> name the exact failure
-> add one missing capability
-> rerun the same job
```

Writing plans now for sessions, compaction, career graphs, watches, project workspaces, or sub-agents would ignore that decision and recreate the earlier architecture drift.

After Plan 002, read its report and choose one next plan from observed evidence. Possible outcomes include:

- conversational correction if follow-up handling is the next failure
- one saved decision if the user-response handoff is the next failure
- source normalization if retrieval quality is the next failure
- context selection if the career fixture is the next failure
- no new primitive if the current result is already good enough

## Considered and rejected for this wave

- **Refactor the existing `src/haxjobs/agent/` loop first**: rejected because it has old callers and would mix migration with the experiment.
- **Delete the old runtime in Plan 001**: rejected because that expands the first model-call experiment into a product migration.
- **Use PydanticAI immediately**: rejected by the decided stack. Reconsider only after local plumbing grows enough that the framework deletes meaningful tested code.
- **Add web search with source inspection**: rejected. Stage 1 measures one tool.
- **Add a database for run traces**: rejected. Local JSONL and Markdown are the decided floor.
- **Add OpenTelemetry or Phoenix**: rejected until local traces become hard to inspect or runs cross processes.
- **Plan Stage 2 and Stage 3 now**: rejected until Stage 0 and Stage 1 traces identify the next real failure.
- **Let reviewers edit quick fixes**: rejected. One Pro writer owns the worktree; Flash reviewers stay independent and read-only.
- **Use the current GPT subagent models as substitutes**: rejected by the user's explicit DeepSeek requirement.

## Reconciliation checklist before `execute`

- [ ] Intended cleanup and discussion state committed.
- [ ] Canonical checkout clean or approved disjoint dirt recorded.
- [ ] Plan files updated to the new planned-at SHA.
- [ ] Current-state excerpts checked against live code.
- [ ] Existing verification baseline recorded.
- [ ] `deepseek-v4-pro` proved available without fallback.
- [ ] Three independent `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions proved available without fallback.
- [ ] Isolated worktree can be created from the committed base.
- [ ] No secret or private runtime path is passed into reviewer contexts.
- [ ] Plan 001 status changed from BLOCKED to TODO.
