content.text:
228	## Stage 0: bare observed model call
   229	
   230	Minimum pieces:
   231	
   232	1. one model-client function
   233	2. one stable Hax instruction block
   234	3. one task-specific instruction block for reviewing a job
   235	4. one current context bundle containing the user request, career fixture, and job fixture
   236	5. one model call
   237	6. one saved run record
   238	7. one human evaluation note
   239	
   240	No tools. No sessions. No compaction. No sub-agents. No skill system. No scheduler. No database.
   241	
   242	This stage tells us what the configured model does when given a clean, source-labelled desk.
   243	
   244	## Stage 1: bounded agent loop with one source tool
   245	
   246	Add only after Job 328 proves the need:
   247	
   248	- tool schema and registry
   249	- one restricted `inspect_job_source(job_ref)` action
   250	- maximum tool-step count
   251	- structured tool result with source, observation time, status, content, and failure
   252	- tool-call trace
   253	
   254	The tool performs normal retrieval code. The model decides whether it needs the source.
   255	
   256	If LinkedIn blocks access or the role is gone, that failure is a valid result. Hax should say the role cannot currently be verified rather than filling the gap with guesses.
   257	
   258	## Stage 2: conversational correction
   259	
   260	Add message history only after testing a follow-up such as:
   261	
   262	> "I actually have three years of Java experience from before university."
   263	
   264	This tests:
   265	
   266	- whether the conversation remains natural
   267	- whether Hax accepts a clear user correction
   268	- whether self-report and evidence strength stay separate
   269	- whether the revised answer uses the correction without rewriting the original job evidence
   270	
   271	Durable cross-session memory can wait. First prove correct handling inside one short conversation.
   272	
   273	## Stage 3: one saved product decision
   274	
   275	Add one narrow internal write after the user says:
   276	
   277	> "Yeah, skip it."
   278	
   279	The result should record the user's decision against the reviewed job and career context.
   280	
   281	This is the first product effect. It should be ordinary typed code, checked after writing. It does not need a general workflow engine.
   282	
   283	# The smallest harness shape
   284	
   285	```text
   286	CLI test runner
   287	    |
   288	    v
   289	Hax run controller
   290	    |-- build stable instructions
   291	    |-- load one fixture bundle
   292	    |-- select allowed tools for this experiment
   293	    |-- call model through one seam
   294	    |-- validate and dispatch requested tools
   295	    |-- stop on final answer or step limit
   296	    |-- save trace and result
   297	    v
   298	Model + narrow normal-code actions
   299	```
   300	
   361	The fixture's old evaluation remains hidden from Hax. We use it only as evidence of what went wrong before.
   362	
   363	# What is a tool in the first experiment
   364	
   365	A tool is an action Hax may need during the current run.
   366	
   367	The likely first tool is:
   368	
   369	```text
   370	inspect_job_source(job_ref)
   371	```
   372	
   373	The action resolves the trusted source URL from the supplied fixture or saved job record. The model does not receive arbitrary network-fetch authority.
   374	
   375	It should return:
   376	
   377	- whether retrieval succeeded
   378	- final resolved URL
   379	- observed time
   380	- source type
   381	- current liveness when detectable
   382	- bounded page content or structured job fields
   383	- warnings and failure code
   384	
   385	It must not return a confident fit decision. Retrieval is normal code. Judgement remains with Hax.
   386	
   387	The following are not tools yet:
   388	
   389	- career profile text, because the context assembler supplies the selected slice
   390	- the job fixture, because the current task already supplies it
   391	- a company-watch workflow, because repeated monitoring is not part of the first run
   392	- a skill catalogue, because no repeated successful procedure has been observed
   393	- sub-agent spawning, because one job review is not context-heavy enough
   394	
   395	# Commands, workflows, and skills
   396	
   397	We should discover these from usage instead of naming a large catalogue now.
   398	
   399	## Command
   400	
   401	A command is how a person or test starts a known action.
   402	
   403	First candidate:
   404	
   405	```text
   406	haxjobs experiment review-job --fixture job-49
   407	```
   408	
   409	The exact syntax can wait until code inspection. The requirement is one repeatable way to run the same fixture with the same inputs.
   410	
   411	## Workflow
   412	
   413	A workflow is controller-owned order that must not be skipped.
   414	
   415	Stage 0 needs almost none:
   416	
   417	```text
   418	load fixture -> build context -> call model -> save trace -> stop
   419	```
   420	
   421	Stage 1 adds bounded tool dispatch. Company monitoring remains later.
   422	
   423	## Skill
   424	
   425	A skill is a saved procedure learned from repeated successful work.
   426	
   427	We should not write a `review-job` skill before seeing whether the stable instructions and flow prompt are enough. If repeated runs show the model consistently misses the same ordered method, that method may later become a skill.
   428	
   429	The promotion rule is:
   430	
   431	```text
   432	one successful prompt trick: keep it in the experiment
   433	repeated stable procedure: consider a skill
   434	mandatory safe ordering: put it in normal workflow code
   435	one narrow action: make it a tool
   436	```
   437	
   438	# Observability for the first run
   439	
   440	We cannot refine what we cannot inspect.
   500	- application packs
   501	- outreach
   502	- application submission
   503	- browser UI
   504	- multi-provider fallback
   505	- automatic self-improvement
   506	- generalized workflow engine
   507	- final database schema
   508	
   509	These are not rejected. They need a failure or a proven next task before entering the minimal harness.
   510	
   511	# How refinement should work
   512	
   513	After each run, write four things:
   514	
   515	1. what Hax received
   516	2. what Hax did
   517	3. what was wrong or surprisingly good
   518	4. the smallest harness change that could explainably improve it
   519	
   520	Then rerun the same fixture.
   521	
   522	Examples:
   523	
   524	| Observed failure | Likely harness change |
   525	|---|---|
   526	| Invents job requirements | Tighten source labels or evidence instruction; add claim check |
   527	| Misses a relevant project | Improve context selection, not the model prompt first |
   528	| Calls source tool unnecessarily | Improve tool description or tool availability for the flow |
   529	| Repeats unsupported sponsorship claim | Add scoped evidence rules and a regression case |
   530	| Becomes confused after follow-up | Fix history assembly before adding durable memory |
   531	| Produces a good method repeatedly | Consider promoting the method into a skill |
   532	| Skips a mandatory safety step | Move that step into controller code, not another reminder |
   533	
   534	This is the HaxJobs version of the evolution primitive. The change follows a repeatable failure and keeps a regression case.
   535	
   536	# Current recommendations
   537	
   538	1. Freeze one truthful backend career-context fixture before code.
   539	2. Run Job 49 first as the clear-evidence control case.
   540	3. Run Job 328 second as the missing-evidence stress case.
   541	4. Start Stage 0 with no tools and save the exact run.
   542	5. Add only `inspect_job_source(job_ref)` for Stage 1.
   543	6. Use the configured real model, with no provider fallback.
   544	7. Save local run artifacts and human review notes.
   545	8. Keep the web app, company watching, database redesign, sessions, compaction, skills, and sub-agents out of the first build.
   546	
   547	# Questions that need Arinze's judgement
   548	
   549	1. **Fixture order:** Decided. Run Job 49 first as the clear-evidence control case, then Job 328 as the case that should force source inspection.
   550	2. **Career fixture:** Decided. Create one frozen backend-career fixture from the user's real current profile and evidence, with private source references kept local.
   551	3. **Build staircase:** Decided. Stage 0 deliberately has no tools. Stage 1 adds only `inspect_job_source(job_ref)` so the effect of the tool is visible.
   552	
   553	# Decision ledger
   554	
   555	| Item | Status | Current direction |
   556	|---|---|---|
   557	| Active design method | Corrected | Build the smallest Hax harness, run real fixtures, observe, and refine one primitive at a time. |
   558	| Previous company-watch modelling | Paused | Keep accepted behaviour as input, but do not treat its proposed domain model as the architecture. |
   559	| Shared harness spine | Working recommendation | One interface-independent run controller around one model boundary. |
   560	| Job-native difference | Working recommendation | Career context, evidence, source freshness, decisions, outcomes, and approval-bound external effects shape each primitive. |
   561	| First task | Working recommendation | Conversational review of one real job against one frozen career context. |
   562	| First fixture | Decided | Job 49 first, then Job 328. |
   563	| First tool | Decided | None in Stage 0, then only `inspect_job_source(job_ref)` in Stage 1. |
   564	| Career fixture | Decided | A frozen slice of the user's real backend track and evidence. |
   565	| Persistence | Working recommendation | Local run trace and Markdown evaluation only for the first experiment. |
   566	| Skills and sub-agents | Deferred | Add only after repeated work proves a stable procedure or isolation need. |
   567	| Database and UI | Deferred | Not needed to learn from the first runs. |
   568	
   569	# Next step
   570	
   571	Decide the language, small framework set, observability floor, and verification floor for the experiment. Then inspect only the current model boundary and configuration needed for Stage 0.
   255	Start plain. Switch when PydanticAI can delete tested plumbing rather than merely replacing ten obvious lines with its own vocabulary.
   256	
   257	### PydanticAI activation condition
   258	
   259	Reconsider it when the local loop has accumulated several of these:
   260	
   261	- provider normalization
   262	- streaming
   263	- retry handling
   264	- usage limits
   265	- deferred approval continuation
   266	- message processors
   267	- toolset composition
   268	- tracing instrumentation
   269	- substantial fake-model plumbing
   270	
   271	The test is simple:
   272	
   273	> Would PydanticAI remove meaningful code while preserving HaxJobs-owned state, policy, and product actions?
   274	
   275	If yes, use it. If it only makes the code look more agent-like, do not.
   276	
   277	## OpenAI Agents SDK
   278	
   279	Good parts:
   280	
   281	- compact agent and runner model
   282	- tools, handoffs, sessions, guardrails, and tracing
   283	- strong OpenAI Responses support
   284	- multiple-provider routes exist
   285	
   286	Costs:
   287	
   288	- OpenAI concepts remain the centre
   289	- hosted tracing creates privacy and provider coupling
   290	- server-managed conversation and compaction can become hidden application state
   291	- HaxJobs would inherit provider-shaped choices too early
   292	
   293	Use it only if OpenAI Responses becomes an intentional primary platform and its realtime, hosted traces, handoffs, or server-managed state are product requirements.
   294	
   295	HaxJobs currently uses DeepSeek. That makes an OpenAI-centred framework a strange default.
   296	
   297	## LangChain
   298	
   299	LangChain is useful when an...

892	### Extend `tests/interfaces/test_experiment_cli.py`
   893	
   894	Add:
   895	
   896	- Stage 0 remains default
   897	- Stage 1 accepted
   898	- invalid max-turns rejected
   899	- no arbitrary URL option exists
   900	- fake Stage 1 trajectory
   901	- output prints safe tool count and artifact path
   902	
   903	No test snapshots full natural language and no unit test uses live HTTP.
   904	
   905	## Done criteria
   906	
   907	All must hold:
   908	
   909	- [ ] Plan 001 is accepted and its Job 328 evidence gate is satisfied.
   910	- [ ] Plan 002 starts from the exact accepted Plan 001 SHA.
   911	- [ ] One DeepSeek v4 Pro writer implements in an isolated worktree.
   912	- [ ] No substitute or fallback writer model is used.
   913	- [ ] Stage 0 and Stage 1 use the same agent-core runner.
   914	- [ ] Model messages remain provider-neutral outside the adapter.
   915	- [ ] Exactly one tool is registered and active for Stage 1.
   916	- [ ] The tool schema accepts `job_ref` and no URL.
   917	- [ ] The model cannot select a different job source.
   918	- [ ] Tool arguments and handler outputs pass Pydantic validation.
   919	- [ ] Unknown, inactive, malformed, failed, over-budget, and truncated calls become structured failures.
   920	- [ ] Assistant tool-call messages are stored before tool results.
   921	- [ ] Tool results retain assistant source order.
   922	- [ ] Default max turns is 3 and max tool executions is 1.
   923	- [ ] Limit exit is explicit and never presented as completion.
   924	- [ ] Public-source and redirect checks run before retrieval.
   925	- [ ] Source content is capped at 500,000 bytes and 12,000 visible characters.
   926	- [ ] Source content is labelled untrusted in model context.
   927	- [ ] Source inspection returns evidence only, never a fit decision.
   928	- [ ] Unit tests make no public network calls.
   929	- [ ] All Plan 001 tests remain green.
   930	- [ ] Full surviving tests, compilation, cron syntax, lock check, and diff check pass.
   931	- [ ] Manual fake Stage 0 and Stage 1 commands run.
   932	- [ ] Configured-provider Job 49 and Job 328 Stage 1 runs complete.
   933	- [ ] Job 49 tool-call count is reviewed; any unnecessary call is reported honestly.
   934	- [ ] Job 328 uses one correctly scoped source call or the report explains a model-choice failure.
   935	- [ ] Retrieval failure never becomes invented evidence.
   936	- [ ] Both Stage 1 human rubrics are complete and accepted.
   937	- [ ] No private fixture, raw trace, fetched page content, answer, or credential is tracked.
   938	- [ ] `diagram/004-stage-1-source-tool-loop.drawio` parses and exports.
   939	- [ ] The tracked PNG matches the current diagram by fresh export and visual review.
   940	- [ ] `diagram/README.md` indexes Plan 002's current-state diagram.
   941	- [ ] The tracked delivery report contains real evidence and manual commands.
   942	- [ ] Three independent DeepSeek v4 Flash reviewers approve the same candidate SHA.
   943	- [ ] No out-of-scope file changed.
   944	- [ ] Candidate worktree is clean after commit.
   945	- [ ] Source checkout status fingerprint is unchanged by execution.
   946	
   947	## STOP conditions
   948	
   949	Stop and report instead of improvising if:
   950	
   951	- Plan 001 is not accepted on one candidate SHA
   952	- Job 328 Stage 0 evidence does not show source inspection as the next missing capability
   953	- the Plan 001 delivery report or local rubric is missing
   954	- the code has drifted from the expected four-layer Stage 0 shape
   955	- any in-scope path has unowned changes
   956	- `deepseek-v4-pro` or three independent `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions are unavailable without fallback
   957	- exact model identity cannot be proved through runtime metadata
   958	- source inspection requires login, cookies, browser automation, CAPTCHA handling, or private credentials
   959	- the tool would accept an arbitrary model-supplied URL
   960	- the tool needs a second source or search tool
   961	- a unit test needs public network access
   962	- safe redirect and private-address handling cannot be tested deterministically
   963	- a trace, report, or tracked file contains private career content or fetched source text
   964	- implementation creates a second loop or imports `haxjobs.agent`
   965	- work expands into sessions, database, monitoring, applications, coding tools, or UI
   966	- Stage 0 tests regress
   967	- a step fails verification twice after one reasonable correction
   968	- Draw.io export fails after import-safe simplification
   969	- reviewer findings conflict with decided product requirements
   970	- three review/fix rounds do not reach one approved candidate
   971	
   972	## Maintenance notes
   973	
   974	- The source tool is an experiment capability, not a general browser.
   975	- A LinkedIn block is evidence about source accessibility, not a failed implementation by itself.
   976	- Keep the active job reference in operation context so future sources cannot be selected through model arguments.
   977	- Do not add retries until traces show a repeatable transient-failure need and retry safety is defined.
   978	- Do not add parallel tools until several independent reads create a measured bottleneck.
   979	- Stage 2 conversational correction and Stage 3 saved decision are intentionally not planned yet. The Plan 002 report must recommend the next plan from observed traces rather than preselecting one.
   980	- The legacy agent path remains separate and should be deleted or migrated only through a later explicit cleanup plan.
   277	## Considered and rejected for this wave
   278	
   279	- **Refactor the existing `src/haxjobs/agent/` loop first**: rejected because it has old callers and would mix migration with the experiment.
   280	- **Delete the old runtime in Plan 001**: rejected because that expands the first model-call experiment into a product migration.
   281	- **Use PydanticAI immediately**: rejected by the decided stack. Reconsider only after local plumbing grows enough that the framework deletes meaningful tested code.
   282	- **Add web search with source inspection**: rejected. Stage 1 measures one tool.
   283	- **Add a database for run traces**: rejected. Local JSONL and Markdown are the decided floor.
   284	- **Add OpenTelemetry or Phoenix**: rejected until local traces become hard to inspect or runs cross processes.
   285	- **Plan Stage 2 and Stage 3 now**: rejected until Stage 0 and Stage 1 traces identify the next real failure.
   286	- **Let reviewers edit quick fixes**: rejected. One Pro writer owns the worktree; Flash reviewers stay independent and read-only.
   287	- **Use the current GPT subagent models as substitutes**: rejected by the user's explicit DeepSeek requirement.
   288	
   289	## Reconciliation checklist before `execute`
   290	
   291	- [ ] Intended cleanup and discussion state committed.
   292	- [ ] Canonical checkout clean or approved disjoint dirt recorded.
   293	- [ ] Plan files updated to the new planned-at SHA.
   294	- [ ] Current-state excerpts checked against live code.
   295	- [ ] Existing verification baseline recorded.
   296	- [ ] `deepseek-v4-pro` proved available without fallback.
   297	- [ ] Three independent `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions proved available without fallback.
   298	- [ ] Isolated worktree can be created from the committed base.
   299	- [ ] No secret or private runtime path is passed into reviewer contexts.
   300	- [ ] Plan 001 status changed from BLOCKED to TODO.

text:
1	# Handoff Plan Template
     2	
     3	Every plan is written for an executor model that has **zero context**: it has not seen the advisor session, the audit, the other plans, or any prior conversation. It may be a smaller/cheaper model. Assume it is competent at following explicit instructions and weak at filling gaps, recovering from ambiguity, or knowing when to stop.
     4	
     5	Three properties make a plan executable by a weaker model:
     6	
     7	1. **Self-contained context** — everything needed is in the file: paths, code excerpts, conventions, commands.
     8	2. **Verification gates** — every step ends with a command and its expected result. The executor never has to *judge* whether it succeeded.
     9	3. **Hard boundaries and escape hatches** — explicit out-of-scope list, and "STOP and report" conditions instead of letting the model improvise when reality doesn't match the plan.
    10	
    11	File naming: `plans/NNN-short-slug.md`, numbered in recommended execution order.
    12	
    13	---
    14	
    15	## Template
    16	
    17	```markdown
    18	# Plan NNN: <Imperative title — what will be true after this plan>
    19	
    20	> **Executor instructions**: Follow this plan step by step. Run every
    21	> verification command and confirm the expected result before moving to the
    22	> next step. If anything in the "STOP conditions" section occurs, stop and
    23	> report — do not improvise. When done, update the status row for this plan
    24	> in `plans/README.md` — unless a reviewer dispatched you and told you they
    25	> maintain the index.
    26	>
    27	> **Drift check (run first)**: `git diff --stat <planned-at SHA>..HEAD -- <in-scope paths>`
    28	> If any in-scope file changed since this plan was written, compare the
    29	> "Current state" excerpts against the live code before proceeding; on a
    30	> mismatch, treat it as a STOP condition.
    31	
    32	## Status
    33	
    34	- **Priority**: P1 | P2 | P3
    35	- **Effort**: S | M | L
    36	- **Risk**: LOW | MED | HIGH
    37	- **Depends on**: plans/NNN-*.md (or "none")
    38	- **Category**: bug | security | perf | tests | tech-debt | migration | dx | docs | direction
    39	- **Planned at**: commit `<short SHA>`, <YYYY-MM-DD>
    40	- **Issue**: <GitHub issue URL — only when published via `--issues`; omit otherwise>
    41	
    42	## Why this matters
    43	
    44	2–5 sentences. The problem, its concrete cost, and what improves when this
    45	lands. Written so the executor (and a human reviewer) understands the intent —
    46	intent is what lets a correct judgment call happen when a detail is off.
    47	
    48	## Current state
    49	
    50	The facts the executor needs, inlined — never "as discussed" or "see audit":
    51	
    52	- The relevant files, each with one line on its role:
    53	  - `src/orders/api.ts` — order-list endpoint; contains the N+1 (lines 130–160)
    54	- Excerpts of the code as it exists today (short, with `file:line` markers),
    55	  enough that the executor can confirm it's looking at the right thing.
    56	- The repo conventions that apply here, with a pointer to one exemplar file:
    57	  "Error handling follows the Result pattern — see `src/lib/result.ts` and its
    58	  use in `src/users/api.ts:40-60`. Match it."
    59	- Any documented vocabulary or design constraints the plan must honor, inlined
    60	  from the intent/design docs found in recon: the relevant `CONTEXT.md` terms
    61	  the executor should use in names and comments, the `DESIGN.md` tokens/components
    62	  to reuse, or the ADR whose decision this work must stay consistent with. Quote
    63	  the specific lines — the executor has not read those docs.
    64	
    65	## Commands you will need
    66	
    67	| Purpose   | Command                  | Expected on success |
    68	|-----------|--------------------------|---------------------|
    69	| Install   | `pnpm install`           | exit 0              |
    70	| Typecheck | `pnpm typecheck`         | exit 0, no errors   |
    71	| Tests     | `pnpm test -- <filter>`  | all pass            |
    72	| Lint      | `pnpm lint`              | exit 0              |
    73	
    74	(Exact commands from this repo — verified during recon, not guessed.)
    75	
    76	## Suggested executor toolkit
    77	
    78	(Optional — include only when relevant skills/tools plausibly exist in the
    79	executor's environment. Skip the section otherwise.)
    80	
    81	- Skills the executor should invoke if available, and for what:
    82	  "use `vercel-react-best-practices` when writing the memoization in step 3".
    83	- Reference docs worth reading before starting, by path or URL.
    84	
    85	## Scope
    86	
    87	**In scope** (the only files you should modify):
    88	- `src/orders/api.ts`
    89	- `src/orders/api.test.ts` (create)
    90	
    91	**Out of scope** (do NOT touch, even though they look related):
    92	- `src/orders/legacy-api.ts` — deprecated path, scheduled for deletion;
    93	  changing it wastes effort and risks the v1 clients still pinned to it.
    94	- Any change to the public response shape — clients depend on it.
    95	
    96	## Git workflow
    97	
    98	(Filled from recon — match the repo's observed conventions.)
    99	
   100	- Branch: `advisor/NNN-<slug>` (or the repo's branch-naming convention if one is evident)
   101	- Commit per step or per logical unit; message style: <match repo, e.g. conventional commits — include an example from `git log`>
   102	- Do NOT push or open a PR unless the operator instructed it.
   103	
   104	## Steps
   105	
   106	### Step 1: <imperative title>
   107	
   108	What to do, precisely. Reference exact files/symbols. Include the target code
   109	shape when it's load-bearing (the pattern to produce, not necessarily every
   110	line).
   111	
   112	**Verify**: `<command>` → <expected output>
   113	
   114	### Step 2: ...
   115	
   116	(Each step small enough to verify independently. Order steps so the codebase
   117	is never broken between steps when possible — e.g. add new path, switch
   118	callers, then remove old path.)
   119	
   120	## Test plan
   121	
   122	- New tests to write, in which file, covering which cases (list them:
   123	  happy path, the specific bug/regression this plan fixes, named edge cases).
   124	- Which existing test to use as the structural pattern:
   125	  "model after `src/users/api.test.ts`".
   126	- Verification: `<test command>` → all pass, including N new tests.
   127	
   128	## Done criteria
   129	
   130	Machine-checkable. ALL must hold:
   131	
   132	- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` exits 0
   133	- [ ] `pnpm test` exits 0; new tests for <X> exist and pass
   134	- [ ] `grep -rn "<old pattern>" src/` returns no matches
   135	- [ ] No files outside the in-scope list are modified (`git status`)
   136	- [ ] `plans/README.md` status row updated
   137	
   138	## STOP conditions
   139	
   140	Stop and report back (do not improvise) if:
   141	
   142	- The code at the locations in "Current state" doesn't match the excerpts
   143	  (the codebase has drifted since this plan was written).
   144	- A step's verification fails twice after a reasonable fix attempt.
   145	- The fix appears to require touching an out-of-scope file.
   146	- You discover the assumption "<key assumption>" is false.
   147	
   148	## Maintenance notes
   149	
   150	For the human/agent who owns this code after the change lands:
   151	
   152	- What future changes will interact with this (e.g. "if pagination is added
   153	  to this endpoint, the batching in step 2 must be revisited").
   154	- What a reviewer should scrutinize in the PR.
   155	- Any follow-up explicitly deferred out of this plan (and why).
   156	```
   157	
   158	---
   159	
   160	## Index file: `plans/README.md`
   161	
   162	Written once by the advisor after all plans, updated by executors:
   163	
   164	```markdown
   165	# Implementation Plans
   166	
   167	Generated by the improve skill on <date>. Execute in the order below unless
   168	dependencies say otherwise. Each executor: read the plan fully before starting,
   169	honor its STOP conditions, and update your row when done.
   170	
   171	## Execution order & status
   172	
   173	| Plan | Title | Priority | Effort | Depends on | Status |
   174	|------|-------|----------|--------|------------|--------|
   175	| 001  | ...   | P1       | S      | —          | TODO   |
   176	| 002  | ...   | P1       | M      | 001        | TODO   |
   177	
   178	Status values: TODO | IN PROGRESS | DONE | BLOCKED (with one-line reason) | REJECTED (with one-line rationale — finding fixed independently or approach abandoned)
   179	
   180	## Dependency notes
   181	
   182	- 002 requires 001 because <reason>.
   183	
   184	## Findings considered and rejected
   185	
   186	- <finding>: not worth doing because <one line>. (So nobody re-audits it.)
   187	```
   188	
   189	## Quality bar — check before finishing each plan
   190
